question: Did the model select Option A as the final answer?
Did the model provide the correct numerical value for sin∠A, which is 3√13/13 (or equivalently 3/√13)?
Did the model correctly identify that ∠C = 90° implies triangle ABC is a right-angled triangle with AC ⊥ BC?
Did the model correctly use the condition BD = AB (with D on the extension of AB) to derive a geometric relationship, such as AD = 2AB or a similarity ratio of 1:2?
Did the model employ a valid geometric construction (e.g., drawing BE // AC) or coordinate method to utilize the condition tan∠DCB = 1/3?
Did the reasoning correctly deduce the ratio between the legs of the right triangle ABC (specifically AC : BC = 2 : 3)?
Did the model correctly calculate the hypotenuse AB (or its proportional value) using the Pythagorean theorem (e.g., AB = √13x)?
Did the model correctly define sin∠A as the ratio of the opposite side to the hypotenuse (BC / AB)?
Is the reasoning process free from logical hallucinations, such as incorrectly assuming triangle BCD is a right triangle without proof?
Are the vertex labels (A, B, C, D) clearly legible and positioned near their corresponding vertices in the generated image?
Is the angle ∠ACB visually depicted as a right angle (approximately 90 degrees) in the generated image?
Does the generated image accurately depict point D lying on the extension of segment AB (i.e., A, B, and D appear collinear in the order A-B-D)?
If the model's solution relies on an auxiliary line (e.g., BE), is this specific line clearly visible in the generated image?
If the model constructs parallel or perpendicular lines (e.g., BE // AC), do these lines appear visually parallel or perpendicular in the image?
Does the generated image visually support the geometric constructions described in the text (e.g., if text says 'draw BE parallel to AC', is BE shown)?
Is the position of point D in the image consistent with the text description (i.e., on the extension of AB rather than between A and B)?
